summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Caddy's templates middleware processes Go template directives in served files. A file containing {{env "EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET"}} served through the templates middleware exposes the value of the EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET environment variable in the HTTP response body, demonstrating unrestricted environment variable access.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the templates middleware to evaluate {{env "EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET"}} and include the secret environment variable value in the HTTP response body, proving that any process environment variable can be exfiltrated through template injection.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Caddy reproduction image, started Caddy with the templates middleware and file_server, sent an HTTP request for /index.html containing {{env "EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET"}}, and received the actual value of EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET in the response body (e.g., evohunt-poc-secret-1780658064), confirming the environment variable was leaked.
verification_method: The verify.sh script sets a unique proof string as EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET, starts Caddy, requests the exploit page via HTTP, and checks whether the response body contains the exact proof string. This distinguishes a real environment variable leak from a generic template error, an empty response, or an unprocessed template directive (which would show the literal {{env ...}} text).
evidence_produced_by_run: The HTTP response body contains the exact value of EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET, not the template directive {{env "EVOHUNT_TEST_SECRET"}}.
The verify.sh script confirmed the proof string was present in the response, ruling out a false positive from template parsing errors.
Caddy started successfully with templates middleware enabled and processed the file as a Go template, ruling out a startup failure.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
